rulings.Case Title: ANDREW D FILIPEK VS. BRIAN DILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order To File Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 21, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF ANDREW FILIPEK's Motion For Order To File Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc. Plaintiff Andrew Filipek's motion for order to file application for and renewal of judgment nunc pro tunc is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The court will sign the proposed order previously submitted by Mr. Filipek. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 21, 2025 Line 3. DEFENDANT SHUZHEN TU's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Shuzhen Tu's motion to quash service of summons and set aside entry of default is denied. Yu failed to provide credible evidence that she could be served at the address listed on her purported Chinese National Identification Card and thus there is no basis to quash the service of summons by publication. Tu's requests to quash the service of summons filed in 2022 and to set aside the entry of default in 2022 are untimely and also lack credible evidentiary support. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LI LING CAI ET AL VS. WENDELL LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Exclude Expert Testimony And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 21, 2025 Line 13. PLAINTIFFs LI CAI, XING TANG, SONG TAN, YUE XU, YONG TANG,and VINNA TANG's Motion To Exclude Expert Testimony And Sanctions. Plaintiffs' motion to exclude the expert testimony of Dr. Gershwein and the trial testimony of Kevin Curran and for sanctions is denied without prejudice to making the same requests to the trial judge. Although labeled a discovery motion, this motion is much more akin to a motion in limine than it is to a discovery motion. Unlike a discovery motion, the motion does not seek to compel or prevent the taking of any discovery. Like a motion in limine, the motion seeks to exclude evidence at trial. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK CHENG VS. VIAL HEALTH TECHNOLOGY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And For Leave To Defend Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, March 21, 2025 Line 12. DEFENDANT SIMON BURNS' Motion To Set Aside Default And For Leave To Defend Action. Defendant Simon Burns' motion to set aside default and for leave to defend action is granted. The default is set aside and Mr. Burns must file a response to the complaint no later than April 11, 2025. Mr. Burns has satisfactorily shown that he did not have actual notice of the service on him by publication and that his lack of notice was not due to any effort on his part to avoid service or any other conduct that would make it inequitable to grant this motion. Granting the motion is in accord with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)
